source.title: Neural Mechanisms of Cognitive Dissonance (Revised): An EEG Study

confidence_rationale: EEG study with proper control showing a frontocentral negative deflection at ~60 ms post-response that resembles error-related negativity. Amplitude correlates with subsequent preference-change magnitude. Rest-state long-range temporal correlations in frontocentral cortex predict both the evoked response and the preference change. Source quality good (J Neurosci, peer-reviewed); mapping is one step removed (ERN-like signal at the choice, not at the post-commit revision attempt) but supports the architectural picture: the act of committing engages performance-monitoring circuitry whose strength predicts how much the agent's representation will subsequently shift.
failure_signatures: The signal is locked to the response (the commit), not to a later attempt to revise the just-committed belief; this paper does not directly observe the revision-cost asymmetry the brief targets, only the upstream commit-monitoring signal.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The post-decisional reevaluation of choice alternatives is preceded by a frontocentral evoked response with the spatial and temporal characteristics of error-related negativity (~60 ms post-response, frontocentral midline), and the amplitude of this response predicts the magnitude of subsequent preference change.
mapping.ree_translation: Neural support for the proposal that crossing the commit boundary engages performance-monitoring circuitry whose engagement intensity predicts how much the agent's downstream representations will be reshaped. Maps to dACC / SMA in the existing REE control-plane signal map. The ERN-like signature at commit suggests the architecture treats the commit as a performance event, which is consistent with the typed-commit-boundary framing of INV-021.
mapping.mapping_caveat: ERN is canonically a response-monitoring signal; its appearance here is interpreted as dissonance-monitoring by analogy. The revision-cost asymmetry the brief targets is the downstream consequence, not the directly measured signal. Resting-state long-range temporal correlations as an individual-difference predictor are interesting but the mechanism by which they confer dissonance susceptibility is not established.
mapping.source_context: Healthy young adults; free-choice paradigm; EEG with rest-state baseline; Russia.

confidence_components.notes: J Neurosci is solid; the mapping to the proposed mechanism is one inferential step (ERN at commit predicts later representational shift, the brief proposes that commits drive lasting representational shifts). Transfer risk modest; same paradigm and demographic as Izuma 2010.
